186→ """ 187→ # Lazy import to avoid circular dependency through erk.cli.commands.run.__init__ 188→ from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor 189→ 190→ from erk.cli.commands.run.shared import extract_plan_number, extract_pr_number 191→ 192→ _PER_WORKFLOW_LIMIT = 20 193→ _MAX_DISPLAY_RUNS = 50 194→ _MAX_TITLE_LENGTH = 50 195→ _MAX_BRANCH_LENGTH = 40 196→ 197→ # 1. Fetch workflow runs from each registered workflow in parallel 198→ # Per-workflow queries return only erk runs (not CI/lint noise). 199→ # Filter by current user so multi-contributor repos show only your runs. 200→ _, gh_username, _ = self._ctx.github.check_auth_status() 201→ tagged_runs: list[tuple[WorkflowRun, str]] = [] 202→ 203→ def _fetch_workflow(cmd_name: str, workflow_file: str) -> list[tuple[WorkflowRun, str]]: 204→ runs = self._ctx.github.list_workflow_runs( 205→ self._location.root, workflow_file, _PER_WORKFLOW_LIMIT, user=gh_username 206→ ) 207→ return [(run, cmd_name) for run in runs] 208→ 209→ with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=len(WORKFLOW_COMMAND_MAP)) as executor: 210→ futures = { 211→ executor.submit(_fetch_workflow, cmd_name, workflow_file): cmd_name 212→ for cmd_name, workflow_file in WORKFLOW_COMMAND_MAP.items() 213→ } 214→ for future in futures: 215→ tagged_runs.extend(future.result())

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Show deleted pr_utils.py content from git

session.tool_result:
"""Utility functions for PR handling."""
from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import PullRequestInfo
def select_display_pr( prs: list[PullRequestInfo], *, exclude_pr_numbers: set[int] | None, ) -> PullRequestInfo | None: """Select PR to display: prefer open, then merged, then closed.
Excludes PRs matching exclude_pr_numbers so the implementation PR is preferred. Falls back to unfiltered selection if excluding leaves no candidates.
Args: prs: List of PRs sorted by created_at descending (most recent first) exclude_pr_numbers: PR numbers to exclude from selection
Returns: PR to display, or None if no PRs """ candidates = prs if exclude_pr_numbers is not None: filtered = [pr for pr in prs if pr.number not in exclude_pr_numbers] if filtered: candidates = filtered
# Check for open PRs (published or draft)
... [15 lines omitted] ...

session.assistant: Now handle `ci_generate_summaries.py`:
session.tool_result: Found 1 file src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/ci_generate_summaries.py

1→"""Generate CI failure summaries using Haiku. 2→ 3→Fetches failing jobs from a GitHub Actions run, retrieves their logs, 4→sends them to Claude Haiku for summarization, and outputs results 5→in ERK-CI-SUMMARY marker format (consumed by ci_summary_parsing.py). 6→ 7→When --pr-number is provided, summaries are also posted as a PR comment 8→and the ci_summary_comment_id is stored in the plan-header metadata. 9→ 10→Usage: 11→ erk exec ci-generate-summaries --run-id 12345 12→ erk exec ci-generate-summaries --run-id 12345 --pr-number 42 13→ 14→Output: 15→ ERK-CI-SUMMARY markers to stdout, progress messages to stderr 16→ 17→Exit Codes: 18→ 0: Success (even if individual jobs fail to summarize) 19→ 1: Error during execution (e.g., cannot fetch failing jobs) 20→ 21→Examples: 22→ $ erk exec ci-generate-summaries --run-id 12345 --pr-number 42 23→ === ERK-CI-SUMMARY:lint === 24→ - Formatting issues in `src/foo.py` 25→ === /ERK-CI-SUMMARY:lint === 26→""" 27→ 28→from __future__ import annotations 29→ 30→import re
... [437 lines omitted] ...
95→ f"Summarize this CI failure log for job '{job_name}' in 2-5 concise " 96→ f"bullet points. Focus on what failed and why.\n\n{log_content}" 109→ lines = ["## CI Failure Summary", ""] 178→ The comment ID, or None if posting failed 223→ click.echo(f"Failed to update comment, creating new one: {result.stderr}", err=True) 241→ click.echo(f"Failed to post comment: {result.stderr}", err=True) 259→ click.echo("Failed to retrieve comment ID", err=True) 308→ click.echo(f"Failed to read PR issue #{plan_issue}", err=True) 314→ "Failed to update plan-header: plan-header block not found in issue body", 337→ click.echo(f"Failed to update PR issue: {update_result.stderr}", err=True) 353→ Individual job failures don't stop other jobs from being summarized. 363→ '.jobs[] | select(.conclusion == "failure") | "\\(.id)\\t\\(.name)"', 371→ click.echo(f"Error fetching failing jobs: {result.stderr}", err=True) 399→ click.echo("(Log fetch failed)") 422→ summary = "(Summarization failed)" 441→ click.echo(f"Failed to post CI summary comment on PR #{pr_number}", err=True) 449→ """Generate CI failure summaries using Haiku.
session.assistant: Now simplify `ci_generate_summaries.py` — delete `_find_plan_issue_for_pr` and simplify `_post_or_update_comment`:

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param:
